title: Attribute, EventArgs, and Exception type names should have the type being extended
description: Attribute, EventArgs, and Exception type names should end with the type being extendedAnalyze your codeconsistency-identifiablemaintainabilityCode SmellconventionWhy is this an issue?Adherence to the standard naming conventions makes your code not only more readable, but more usable. For instance,class FirstAttribute : Attributecan be used simply withFirst, but you must use the full name forclass AttributeOne : Attribute.This rule raises an issue when classes extendingAttribute,EventArgs, orException, do not end with their parent class names.Noncompliant code exampleclass AttributeOne : Attribute // Noncompliant { }Compliant solutionclass FirstAttribute : Attribute { }ExceptionsIf a class' direct base class doesn’t follow the convention, then no issue is reported on the class itself, regar
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Attribute, EventArgs, and Exception type names should end with the type being extended.
